# EventForge

> Production-ready GPU fleet as a service. No provisioning your own boxes, no fleet management, no manual scaling. Submit jobs over HTTP, receive completions over WebSocket, pay per job.

Canonical site: https://eventforge.loboforge.com/
Agent / operator runbook (repo): event-forge/AGENTS.md
Extended context for AI assistants: https://eventforge.loboforge.com/ai-context/eventforge-context.md
Operator runbook (web): https://eventforge.loboforge.com/ai-context/agents.md
Integration guide (LoboForge monorepo): https://github.com/loboforge/LoboForge.Studio/blob/main/docs/QueueIntegration.md
Operator / coding agent runbook: https://eventforge.loboforge.com/ai-context/agents.md
Sitemap: https://eventforge.loboforge.com/sitemap.xml

## Primary pages

- Home / product overview: https://eventforge.loboforge.com/
- Ops console (authenticated, noindex): https://eventforge.loboforge.com/ops
- AI integration context: https://eventforge.loboforge.com/ai-context/eventforge-context.md

## What EventForge provides

- HTTP job queue (`POST /v1/jobs`) scoped by app API key → `app_id`
- WebSocket event bus (`WSS /v1/ws`) with subscribe + replay for offline clients
- HTTP event replay (`GET /api/v1/events?since=…`) as fallback
- Managed GPU worker fleet (check-in, claim, health, Vast.ai provisioning)
- Artifact upload and storage via EventForge (`PUT /v1/jobs/{id}/output`)
- Ops dashboard for fleet, queue, failures, and Vast rent/terminate

## What integrators do NOT do

- Provision or rent GPU boxes themselves
- Manage worker bootstrap, scaling, or fleet health dashboards (unless using ops console)
- Publish completion events (listen-only WebSocket)
- Write job outputs directly to their own object storage (workers upload through EventForge)

## Event types (WebSocket)

- forge.job.started
- forge.job.completed
- forge.job.failed
- forge.job.timeout
- forge.job.released
- forge.stream.token
- forge.stream.done

## Pricing model

Usage-based per GPU job. Contact ops@loboforge.com for API keys and integration.
